fields.acceptance_criteria: 1. ticket-http exposes workflow-oriented read surfaces for actionable next, blockers, and nested unblocked-by or equivalent unlock-tree payloads. 2. Response arrays preserve canonical workflow order from ticket-api instead of generic list ordering. 3. Responses include the rank and subtree metadata needed by viewer clients, including frontier-leaf counts, blocker distance, dependency-state gap, and recent-unblock timestamps where relevant. 4. Focused HTTP tests cover JSON shape, ordering, and root-scoped workflow queries.
fields.title: [ticket-http] Expose workflow trees and actionable ordering metadata

fields.implementation_summary: Added ticket-http workflow endpoints for actionable next, blocker trees, and unblocked-by trees. The handlers now build transport payloads directly from ticket-api WorkflowModel, include timing metadata and root-scoped recommendation context, and surface frontier_items on tree responses so HTTP clients can render leaf-focused follow-up views without recomputing workflow state.
fields.title: [ticket-http] Expose workflow trees and actionable ordering metadata
fields.validation_summary: cargo test -p ticket-http --no-run; cargo test -p ticket-http workflow_ -- --nocapture

fields.title: [ticket-http] Expose workflow trees and actionable ordering metadata
fields.validation_summary: Previous validation was thin and backend-only: cargo test -p ticket-http --no-run plus cargo test -p ticket-http workflow_ -- --nocapture. Revalidated 2026-05-31: cargo test -p ticket-http workflow_ -- --nocapture passed; Edge-backed Playwright release rerun of e2e-release/workflow-sidebar.spec.ts passed (2 tests).
